macro_events.event_summary: Kevin Warsh, nominated as Fed Chair, faces his Senate confirmation hearing Tuesday, with markets focused on his historically hawkish/hard-money leanings versus pressure to align with the administration's easier-policy preferences.
primary_drivers: Rate path uncertainty — Warsh historically hawkish on inflation but nominated by an administration wanting cuts; hearing tone will shift 2026 cut odds
Fed independence/credibility signaling affecting long-end yields and dollar
Regulatory posture — Warsh viewed as lighter-touch on bank regulation than Powell-era framework

beneficiaries.rationale: Warsh spent years at Morgan Stanley and is viewed as Wall Street-friendly; market expects lighter bank capital/Basel III Endgame enforcement under his Fed. Direct regulatory relief for large broker-dealers with capital markets revenue exposure.
Same thesis as GS — Warsh's former employer, perceived regulatory tailwind for capital markets/trading businesses if he signals softer supervisory stance in testimony.

headlines: Here's what to expect from Fed chair nominee Kevin Warsh's Senate hearing Tuesday

macro_events.event_summary: Associated British Foods (Primark owner) warns that the Iran war is hurting its retail outlook, becoming one of the first European retailers to flag the conflict as a drag on consumer demand and costs.
primary_drivers: Middle East conflict raising energy/freight costs and denting European consumer confidence
Shipping route disruption (Red Sea / Strait of Hormuz risk) lifting logistics costs for apparel importers
Defense spending tailwind from escalating regional war

beneficiaries.rationale: Direct beneficiary of higher crude prices from Iran war risk premium; Strait of Hormuz concerns support Brent, and XOM's upstream leverage is among the highest of US supermajors.
Lockheed sells Patriot, THAAD and PAC-3 missile defense systems being actively consumed/ordered in the Israel-Iran theater; direct order-book tailwind.
Raytheon makes the interceptors (SM-3, SM-6, AMRAAM) being expended in Middle East air defense; restocking orders are a direct revenue driver.
Container shipper that benefits from Red Sea/Hormuz rerouting spiking spot freight rates — same dynamic that lifted ZIM during 2024 Houthi attacks.

losers.rationale: Off-price apparel retailer with business model closest to Primark's; if ABF is flagging weaker apparel demand and higher sourcing/freight costs, TJX faces the same margin pressure on European ops (TK Maxx) and similar consumer headwinds.
Pure-play off-price apparel peer of Primark; a named competitor warning on outlook is a direct read-across for discretionary apparel demand and import freight costs.
European consumer exposure via mass-market apparel — wait, better pick:
Urban Outfitters has meaningful European exposure and discretionary young-consumer skew — the exact cohort Primark serves — so a Primark demand warning is a negative read-across.
Higher jet fuel from Iran war premium directly compresses airline margins; Delta has significant transatlantic exposure that is also demand-sensitive to Middle East travel fears.

headlines: Primark owner warns of impact of war in Iran on outlook — making it one of the first European retailers to do so

macro_events.event_summary: Kevin Warsh, a known hawk and balance sheet critic, faces confirmation hearing (likely for Fed Chair) as a framework for a materially smaller Fed balance sheet emerges.
primary_drivers: Reduced Fed reserve demand / faster QT implies tighter financial conditions and higher term premia
Steeper yield curve / higher long-end rates from reduced Fed Treasury holdings
Hawkish leadership signal reduces expectations of rate cuts and liquidity backstop

beneficiaries.rationale: Higher short rates and steeper curve lift net interest margin on client cash sweep balances; Schwab's earnings are directly levered to the short end staying higher-for-longer under a hawkish Fed regime.
Life insurers benefit from higher long-end yields on reinvestment of fixed income portfolios; a smaller Fed balance sheet and steeper curve directly raises book-yield trajectory.
Large asset-sensitive bank with heavy long-duration securities book; steeper curve from reduced Fed Treasury holdings improves NIM and reduces AOCI drag as existing hedges roll.
Rate volatility and reduced Fed market footprint drives higher Treasury and SOFR futures volumes; CME is the direct toll-taker on any increase in rates hedging activity.

losers.rationale: High-multiple, long-duration tech name trading on terminal value; rising long-end yields from QT framework compresses the multiple mechanically via DCF.

Realty Income and other bond-proxy REITs trade inversely to 10Y yields; a smaller Fed balance sheet framework raises long rates and widens cap rates, hitting NAV and dividend-yield spread attractiveness.

headlines: As Warsh faces hearing, a framework for smaller Fed balance sheet emerges

macro_events.event_summary: Wall Street strategists warn markets may be too complacent about Iran war risks as stocks trade at record highs, suggesting mispriced geopolitical tail risk.
primary_drivers: Underpriced Middle East geopolitical risk premium
Potential oil supply disruption (Strait of Hormuz / Iranian exports)
Possible volatility spike if complacency reverses

beneficiaries.rationale: Largest US integrated oil major with significant upstream leverage; any Iran-related supply disruption or risk premium lifts Brent, directly flowing to E&P earnings. Not Middle East asset-heavy, so limited downside from regional escalation.
Pure-play US E&P with ~100% upstream exposure — highest beta to crude price spike from Iran supply shock. No downstream drag.
Lockheed makes THAAD, PAC-3 missiles and F-35s — direct beneficiaries of increased Middle East defense demand and US munitions restocking if Iran conflict escalates.
Raytheon produces Patriot interceptors and Tomahawks being consumed in Middle East operations; munitions replenishment contracts are a direct earnings tailwind.
Northrop's missile defense and strategic systems are direct beneficiaries of elevated Iran tensions and increased DoD supplemental funding.
US-focused E&P with high oil beta; Buffett-backed name often sees flows on oil risk premium trades. Slightly less pure than COP but higher liquidity in options.

losers.rationale: Delta has highest jet fuel cost sensitivity among US majors; Iran-driven crude spike compresses margins immediately, and Middle East route disruptions add secondary pressure.
United has meaningful international long-haul exposure including routes affected by Middle East airspace closures; fuel cost and rerouting both negative.
Carnival — cruise operator with Mediterranean/Middle East itineraries; fuel costs plus itinerary disruption plus demand hit from conflict headlines. Triple exposure.
Southwest is domestic-only but unhedged on fuel relative to peers historically; pure jet fuel cost shock with no international revenue offset.
Hilton has Middle East franchise exposure and broader travel demand sensitivity to geopolitical shocks; leisure travel bookings historically soften on regional war escalation.

headlines: Wall Street fears 'too much optimism' about Iran war as stocks hit record highs
